title: auth — model relationships
description: Six owned tables plus Sanctum's shipped `personal_access_tokens`. Every table references either `identities` (password resets, verifications, MFA challenges, cross-app grants) or is unrelated to a specific identity (JWT signing keys, JWT deny list). Refresh tokens reference the Sanctum `tokenable` polymorphically (User OR PlatformUser). NO tenant scope on ANY of these tables — tenant / application scope propagates through the referring User / PlatformUser or through the encoded JWT claims.

internal.description: Polymorphic parent — refresh tokens issue against a User (tenant plane) OR a PlatformUser (platform plane). Never against an Identity directly — refresh tokens are session-scoped, not credential-scoped.

Every rotation shares a `family_id` with its predecessor. Reuse detection: if any consumed token in the family is presented again, EVERY token in the family (and every active PAT for the tokenable) is invalidated as a breach signal.
Signing keys are per-Application for `app` claim binding. NULL = global signing key (used for platform-plane JWTs).

outbound.description: Each refresh token pairs with the Sanctum PAT it accompanies. Revoking the PAT cascades the refresh token; consuming the refresh token invalidates the paired PAT.
One-time-use token bound to a specific Identity. Cascade on Identity hard delete.
Email-ownership token — issued at registration + on email change. Cascade on Identity hard delete.
Ephemeral pending-login state (5-minute TTL). Cascade on Identity hard delete.
Grant token carries source + target Application IDs. Restrict on delete so a disappeared Application can't orphan grants mid-exchange.
Grant is scoped to the Identity being cross-authenticated.
jti is a UUID string; the deny list is a lookup table, not a graph node.
Sanctum's shipped polymorphism. Auth::user() resolves to User (sanctum guard) or PlatformUser (platform_admin guard) based on the token.

inbound.description: Every auth-lifecycle mutation is audited via HasActivityLog composition. Audit rows carry the auditable_type / auditable_id polymorphic pointer back.
Product-audit rows record who did what on the auth surface — grant issue/exchange, key rotation, session revocation.

accessor_chain.description: Auth models expose computed accessors that downstream code reads instead of touching hashes / ciphertexts directly.

RefreshToken.is_active: revoked_at IS NULL AND consumed_at IS NULL AND expires_at > now()
RefreshToken.family_size: count of RefreshToken WHERE family_id = self.family_id
RefreshToken.is_breach_family: any RefreshToken in family has consumed_at IS NOT NULL AND presented again — flagged by RefreshTokenObserver

PersonalAccessToken.is_expired: expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at < now()
PersonalAccessToken.abilities_compact: the compact permission array cached at issue-time in the `abilities` column
PersonalAccessToken.application_id_scope: extracted from the token's issuance context (Sanctum's abilities column carries a `_app` pseudo-ability)

JwtSigningKey.is_active: is_active = true AND retired_at IS NULL
JwtSigningKey.is_verify_only: retired_at IS NOT NULL AND retired_at + 180 days > now()
